case_id: VFR Flight Plan KSMF-KRNO with Terrain Analysis

pi_items.verified_value: KSMF - Sacramento International Airport, Sacramento, California
KRNO - Reno/Tahoe International Airport, Reno, Nevada

pi_items.source: Avare help.html (https://raw.githubusercontent.com/apps4av/avare/master/app/src/main/assets/help.html), Avare FAQ (https://www.apps4av.com/faq.html), GitHub releases (https://github.com/apps4av/avare/releases), multiple web searches

pi_items.verified_value: TRUE - Route crosses Sierra Nevada mountains with elevation gain from ~30 feet (Sacramento) to 8,911 feet (Mt. Rose Pass) to 4,400 feet (Reno)
pi_items.source: https://www.flattestroute.com/Sacramento-to-Reno, topographic maps of Sierra Nevada region, USGS terrain elevation data

pi_items.verified_value: N38W120 is a valid SRTM 1°x1° data tile covering 38°N-39°N, 120°W-121°W (includes Sierra Nevada region on route)

privileged_info_summary: The task involves planning a VFR flight from Sacramento (KSMF) to Reno (KRNO) using Avare aviation GPS. Both airports are correctly identified: KSMF is Sacramento International Airport in California, and KRNO is Reno/Tahoe International Airport in Nevada. The route crosses the Sierra Nevada mountains with significant terrain elevation changes, and N38W120 is a valid SRTM terrain data tile covering the region. However, a critical issue exists: the task description requires opening a 'Profile View' via 'Menu > Tools > Profile' to visualize the route's vertical cross-section. This feature does not exist in Avare's documented features. Official Avare help documentation, FAQ, and GitHub releases contain no reference to a 'Profile View' or 'Profile' submenu under Tools. The Tools tab in Avare provides GPS status and data management (IO) functionality, not terrain profiling. This represents a significant contradiction between the task requirements and Avare's actual capabilities.
